title: 4 things to know about Celtics 2nd-round pick Dillon Mitchell
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics drafted Dillon Mitchell, a St. John's forward, with the No. 40 overall pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.
Mitchell is known for his defensive skills and athleticism, but struggles with outside shooting.
Mitchell played for three college programs, ending at St. John's with coach Rick Pitino, reaching the Sweet 16.

title: Dusty Rhodes, executive director of Sail Boston, on what it takes to bring the tall ships to Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Dusty Rhodes leads her fifth tall ships event for Sail Boston, scheduled for weeks away, marking 45 years of event planning.
Boston expects over 60 tall ships from 20 countries in 2026, with an anticipated economic impact of $180 million.
Rhodes credits Boston's harbor design for hosting successful tall ship events, enhancing both visibility and accessibility.

title: Readers share Boston’s top attractions and Ogunquit’s best spots
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Readers recommend must-see Boston attractions like the North End and The Freedom Trail during the World Cup.
Ogunquit, Maine named top New England beach town by readers, highlighting Marginal Way and Barnacle Billy’s.
Hampton Beach Country Music Fest is set for July 7-9; Boston hotels are hosting events for Sail Boston.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (June 25, 2026)
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Chaba Café in Brookline offers Thai street-style desserts and drinks, including coconut ice cream and butterfly pea lemonade.
LPM Restaurant & Bar will open this summer in the Four Seasons Hotel, featuring French-Riviera-themed cuisine.
Naya, a fast-casual Lebanese eatery, opens a new location in Fenway, serving bowls, wraps, and mezze.

title: After a devastating fire, South End condo owners say over $800,000 went missing
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Condo trustees allege over $800,000 intended for reconstruction is missing after South End fire.
The lawsuit claims Premier Property Solutions misappropriated insurance funds meant for reconstruction.
Premier Property Solutions' owner plans to recover lost funds amid ongoing legal and financial reviews.

title: Red Sox continue to get bad news on Roman Anthony’s recovery from lingering wrist injury
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony hasn't played for the Red Sox since May 4 due to a sprained right wrist and hand.
Interim manager Chad Tracy reported Anthony has not made significant progress in his recovery.
Anthony's season statistics include a .229 average with one home run and five RBIs in 30 games.

title: Celtics select St. John’s wing Dillon Mitchell with 40th pick in 2026 NBA Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Celtics selected Dillon Mitchell from St. John's with the 40th pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.
Mitchell, a 6-foot-7 wing, played at Texas, Cincinnati, and St. John's, excelling as a versatile defender.
Despite defensive strengths, Mitchell's offensive potential is hindered by shooting struggles, notably in 3-point and free-throw shooting.

title: Boy, 13, hospitalized after being found unresponsive in swimming pool at Beverly home
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 13-year-old boy was found unresponsive in a pool at a Beverly home and flown to a Boston hospital.
Bystanders performed CPR until first responders arrived; the boy was initially stable at Beverly Hospital.
Beverly police are investigating the incident, which occurred at a home on Parramatta Road.

title: Here’s where every tall ship will be docked for Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 250 will host over 60 tall ships from July 11-16 in Charlestown, downtown Boston, and the Seaport area.
The Parade of Sail on July 11 will feature the USS Constitution, leading a procession of global tall ships.
Public viewing hours for the ships are 10 a.m. to 10 p.m. from July 12-15, with boarding at captain's discretion.

title: Windhorst: ‘Very good chance’ Jaylen Brown isn’t on Celtics next season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is reportedly the subject of active trade talks from the Boston Celtics with multiple interested teams.
The Celtics' interest in trading Brown follows their unsuccessful bid to acquire Giannis Antetokounmpo from the Bucks.
Celtics' President Brad Stevens remained noncommittal about Brown's future, highlighting the team's intention to rework its roster.
